list.innerHTML = cart.map(item => ` ${item.name} ₹${item.price} ${item.qty} ₹${item.price * item.qty} `).join(''); document.getElementById('grandTotal').textContent = '₹' + calcTotal(); } ``` > [!TIP] > **Examiner's Favourite Question:** "Why do you use `reduce` instead of a `for` loop?" > **Model Answer:** "Both produce identical results, but `reduce` expresses the *intent* ('sum these up') declaratively in one line, while a loop forces the reader to track an accumulator variable. Functional style is also easier to chain for GST/discount calculations." --- ## 3.5 The Cross-Project Synthesis Matrix | Concern | Countdown | To-Do | RPS | Shopping Cart | |---------|-----------|-------|-----|----------------| | **State location** | `const target` | `let tasks[]` | `let score{}` | `let cart[]` | | **Persistence** | Not needed | `localStorage` | `localStorage` | `localStorage` | | **Interval?** | ✅ `setInterval` | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | **Random?** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ `Math.random` | ❌ | | **reduce()?** | ❌ | Optional (count pending) | ❌ | ✅ **Grand Total** | | **Re-render trigger** | Every second | Every CRUD | Every move | Every CRUD | # SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics > [!NOTE] > The following Mermaid diagrams are compiled with **alphanumeric node IDs only** and **no markdown formatting inside labels**, per the engine's safeguards. --- ## 4.1 Master Architecture — "Vanilla JS Project Skeleton" ```mermaid flowchart TD A0[User opens index.html] --> B0[Browser parses HTML] B0 --> C0[Browser parses CSS] C0 --> D0[Browser executes script.js] D0 --> E0[Build Model: variables, arrays, objects] E0 --> F0[Bind Event Listeners to DOM] F0 --> G0[Initial render: paint the View] G0 --> H0[App is idle, awaiting events] H0 --> I0[User clicks a button] I0 --> J0[Event Handler fires Controller] J0 --> K0[Mutate Model] K0 --> L0[Persist via localStorage if needed] L0 --> M0[Call render to repaint View] M0 --> H0 ``` --- ## 4.2 Countdown Clock — Temporal State Machine ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> Running: page load, setInterval starts Running --> Running: every 1000 ms, update DOM Running --> Expired: delta less than or equal to 0 Expired --> Expired: display EXPIRED, clearInterval Expired --> [*] ``` --- ## 4.3 To-Do List — CRUD Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD U0[User types in input box] --> U1{Input non empty?} U1 -- No --> U0 U1 -- Yes --> U2[tasks.push newItem] U2 --> U3[localStorage.setItem tasks] U3 --> U4[render repaints list] U4 --> U0 V0[User clicks checkbox] --> V1[toggleTask id] V1 --> V2[save and render] V2 --> U0 W0[User clicks X button] --> W1[deleteTask id] W1 --> W2[filter removes item] W2 --> W3[save and render] W3 --> U0 ``` --- ## 4.4 Rock Paper Scissors — Decision Tree ```mermaid flowchart TD P0[User picks rock paper or scissors] --> P1[Math random times 3, floor to get comp move] P1 --> P2{player equals comp?} P2 -- Yes --> P3[Result = DRAW, increment d] P2 -- No --> P4{diff equals 1?} P4 -- Yes --> P5[Result = WIN, increment w] P4 -- No --> P6[Result = LOSE, increment l] P3 --> P7[Update scoreboard DOM] P5 --> P7 P6 --> P7 P7 --> P8[localStorage.setItem score] P8 --> P0 ``` --- ## 4.5 Shopping Cart — Data Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD S0[User clicks Add to Cart on catalog] --> S1{Item already in cart array?} S1 -- No --> S2[Push new item with qty 1] S1 -- Yes --> S3[Increment existing qty by 1] S2 --> S4[Save cart to localStorage] S3 --> S4 S4 --> S5[render repaints table and total] T0[User clicks plus or minus] --> T1[changeQty id, delta] T1 --> T2{New qty less than or equal to 0?} T2 -- Yes --> T3[Remove item from cart] T2 -- No --> T4[Update qty in place] T3 --> S4 T4 --> S4 R0[reduce loops over cart] --> R1[For each item compute price times qty] R1 --> R2[Accumulate into sum starting at 0] R2 --> R3[Return grand total to render] ``` --- ## 4.6 Comparative Project Map ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph Temporal C[Countdown Clock] end subgraph Persistence T[To Do List] S[Shopping Cart] end subgraph Probability R[Rock Paper Scissors] end C --- T T --- S S --- R R --- C ``` > [!NOTE] > This last diagram is a reminder that the four projects are **not silos** — they share the same controller logic, the same render pattern, and the same persistence story. A student who builds one and *abstracts* the pattern can build the other three in under an hour. # SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap --- ## Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each) ### Q1. `[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Model]` **Explain the role of `JSON.stringify()` and `JSON.parse()` in a To-Do List web application that uses `localStorage`. Why can we not store a JavaScript object directly?** **Model Answer (3 marks):** `localStorage` is a **key-value store that only accepts strings**. When we save a JavaScript array of task objects (the Model), we must first serialize it: $$ \text{storage} \leftarrow \text{localStorage.setItem}(\text{'tasks'},\ \text{JSON.stringify}(\text{tasks})) $$ `JSON.stringify()` walks the object graph and produces a JSON text representation. On page reload, we reverse the process with `JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('tasks'))`, which **rehydrates** the text back into a live JS object. We then defensively default to `[]` if the key is missing: $$ \text{tasks} \leftarrow \text{JSON.parse}(\text{localStorage.getItem}(\text{'tasks'})) \ \vert\vert\ \lbrack\ \rbrack $$ Without `stringify`, `setItem` would coerce the object via `.toString()`, producing the useless string `"[object Object],[object Object]"`. **(3 marks)** --- ### Q2. `[KTU University Exam – June 2024 Model]` **A countdown clock shows 1 hour 75 minutes 90 seconds. What is wrong with the implementation, and how do you fix it using the modulo operator?** **Model Answer (3 marks):** The bug is the **missing modulo operation**. The naive developer computes: $$ \text{minutes} = \left\lfloor \Delta / 60000 \right\rfloor = 135 $$ instead of capping at 60. The fix is: $$ \text{minutes} = \left\lfloor \Delta / 60000 \right\rfloor \bmod 60 = 15 $$ The result is now correctly **1 hour, 15 minutes, 30 seconds**. The modulo operator rolls over overflow into the next higher unit. The same pattern fixes the hours field (mod 24) and the days field. **(3 marks)** --- ## Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each, Internal Choice) > [!IMPORTANT] > Per KTU 2024 ESE regulations, every Part-B question offers an **internal choice** between two questions of comparable cognitive weight. Two full alternative questions are provided below. --- ### Question A (14 Marks) **`[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Model]`** > **(a)** Design the complete JavaScript logic for a **To-Do List** web application that supports adding tasks, marking them as complete (toggle), deleting them, and persisting data across browser refreshes using `localStorage`. Provide the HTML structure and the JavaScript code with proper event listeners. **(7 marks)** > > **(b)** Explain, with a code snippet, how a **Shopping Cart** computes the grand total using `Array.prototype.reduce()`. Show the data model and the render function. **(7 marks)** #### Model Solution — Part (a) — 7 Marks **HTML Structure (1 mark):** ```html
      ``` **JavaScript — Model and Persistence (2 marks):** ```javascript let tasks = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('tasks')) || []; function save() { localStorage.setItem('tasks', JSON.stringify(tasks)); } ``` **JavaScript — Controller Functions (3 marks):** ```javascript function addTask() { const text = document.getElementById('taskInput').value.trim(); if (!text) return; tasks.push({ id: Date.now(), text, done: false }); document.getElementById('taskInput').value = ''; save(); render(); } function toggleTask(id) { const t = tasks.find(x => x.id === id); if (t) t.done = !t.done; save(); render(); } function deleteTask(id) { tasks = tasks.filter(x => x.id !== id); save(); render(); } document.getElementById('addBtn').addEventListener('click', addTask); ``` **Render Function (1 mark):** ```javascript function render() { document.getElementById('taskList').innerHTML = tasks.map(t => `
    • ${t.text}
      • `).join(''); } render(); ``` #### Model Solution — Part (b) — 7 Marks **Data Model (1 mark):** Each cart item is an object `{ id, name, price, qty }`. **The Grand Total Logic (3 marks):** ```javascript function calcTotal() { return cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.price * item.qty), 0); } ``` Explanation: `reduce` walks the array, calling the arrow function with the running `sum` (starting at `0`) and the current `item`. It returns each item's `price * qty` added to the sum. The final return is the grand total. **Worked Example (2 marks):** For cart `[{price: 100, qty: 2}, {price: 50, qty: 3}]`: $$ \text{Total} = (100 \times 2) + (50 \times 3) = 200 + 150 = 350 $$ **Render (1 mark):** Inside `render()`, after building the rows, set: ```javascript document.getElementById('grandTotal').textContent = '₹' + calcTotal(); ``` --- ### Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice **`[KTU University Exam – June 2024 Model]`** > **(a)** Implement a **Countdown Clock** in JavaScript that counts down to a user-specified target date. Show how the remaining time is computed, converted into days/hours/minutes/seconds, and updated every second using `setInterval`. Handle the "expired" state. **(7 marks)** > > **(b)** Implement the **decision engine** for a Rock Paper Scissors game. Explain the cyclic dominance using a mathematical formula and show how the score is persisted. **(7 marks)** #### Model Solution — Part (a) — 7 Marks **HTML (1 mark):** ```html

      00d : 00h : 00m : 00s

      ``` **JavaScript (5 marks):** ```javascript let ticker; document.getElementById('startBtn').addEventListener('click', () => { const target = new Date(document.getElementById('targetInput').value).getTime(); clearInterval(ticker); const $d = (id) => document.getElementById(id); function update() { const delta = target - Date.now(); if (delta <= 0) { clearInterval(ticker); $d('display').textContent = 'EXPIRED'; return; } const pad = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, '0'); $d('display').textContent = pad(Math.floor(delta / 86400000)) + 'd : ' + pad(Math.floor(delta / 3600000) % 24) + 'h : ' + pad(Math.floor(delta / 60000) % 60) + 'm : ' + pad(Math.floor(delta / 1000) % 60) + 's'; } ticker = setInterval(update, 1000); update(); }); ``` **Explanation (1 mark):** `setInterval` ticks every 1000 ms; the `delta` is split using integer division and modulo to extract days/hours/minutes/seconds; on expiry `clearInterval` stops the loop and the UI shows "EXPIRED". #### Model Solution — Part (b) — 7 Marks **Cyclic Dominance Formula (3 marks):** If we map `rock=0, paper=1, scissors=2`, then "player wins" is equivalent to: $$ (\text{player} - \text{comp} + 3) \bmod 3 = 1 $$ This exploits the cyclic ordering. The `+3` ensures the result is non-negative before taking modulo. **The Engine (3 marks):** ```javascript const moves = ['rock', 'paper', 'scissors']; let score = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('rps')) || { w: 0, l: 0, d: 0 }; function play(player) { const comp = Math.floor(Math.random() * 3); const diff = (player - comp + 3) % 3; const result = diff === 0 ? 'draw' : (diff === 1 ? 'win' : 'lose'); if (result === 'win') score.w++; if (result === 'lose') score.l++; if (result === 'draw') score.d++; localStorage.setItem('rps', JSON.stringify(score)); console.log(`You:${moves[player]} Comp:${moves[comp]} → ${result} | W:${score.w} L:${score.l} D:${score.d}`); } ``` **Persistence (1 mark):** Score is stored as a JSON string under the key `rps` and rehydrated on page load with a default `{w:0, l:0, d:0}` fallback. --- ## KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout > [!WARNING] > **Where students lose marks in Module 4 questions:** > > 1. **Forgetting `JSON.stringify`/`parse` (–2 marks):** Writing `localStorage.setItem('cart', cart)` instead of wrapping in `JSON.stringify`. The data silently corrupts to a useless string. > 2. **Off-by-one in `Math.random() * 3` (–1 mark):** Students write `Math.random() * 2 + 1` thinking they need 1, 2, 3 — but `Math.random()` returns `[0, 1)`, so `* 3` gives `[0, 3)`, and `floor` gives `0, 1, 2`. > 3. **Missing modulo (–2 marks):** Producing "75 minutes" instead of "1 hour 15 minutes". > 4. **Not calling `clearInterval` (–1 mark):** The clock keeps ticking after expiry, hammering the DOM and wasting CPU. Examiners explicitly test for this. > 5. **Mixing `splice` and `slice` (–1 mark):** Using `slice` to delete (it does not mutate). > 6. **No default fallback on `localStorage.getItem` (–1 mark):** On the very first visit, the key is `null`; `JSON.parse(null)` returns `null`, and calling `.map` on it throws a `TypeError`. Always write `JSON.parse(...) || []`. --- ## Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember > [!NOTE] > **Final 60-second revision checklist — read this the night before the exam.** - ✅ The four projects (Countdown, To-Do, RPS, Cart) all use the **same MVC-Lite** architecture: Model (JS variable/array) → Controller (event handler) → View (DOM). - ✅ A countdown uses **`Date.now()` + `setInterval(update, 1000)`** and **always** clears the interval on expiry. - ✅ Day/Hour/Minute/Second extraction **requires the modulo operator** (`% 24`, `% 60`) — never skip it. - ✅ `localStorage` stores **strings only**; always pair `setItem` with `JSON.stringify` and `getItem` with `JSON.parse`. - ✅ Always use `JSON.parse(x) || []` (or `|| {}`) — never trust `localStorage` to be populated. - ✅ Random integer in `[0, n)` = `Math.floor(Math.random() * n)`. - ✅ RPS decision: `(player - comp + 3) % 3` — `0` = draw, `1` = win, `2` = lose. - ✅ `Array.reduce((sum, x) => sum + x.field, 0)` is the **canonical** grand-total idiom for the cart. - ✅ `splice` mutates and deletes; `slice` is non-mutating and returns a sub-array. - ✅ `addEventListener` is the modern way to bind; inline `onclick=""` works but is marked down by KTU examiners. - ✅ Always call a single `render()` function after every state mutation — **Single Source of Truth**. - ✅ The To-Do and Cart both use the persistence trinity: **Model → `save()` → `render()`**. - ✅ The exam rarely asks you to write a full project; it asks for **key code segments** (e.g., the `reduce` line, the time-conversion block, the decision formula). Memorize these. - ✅ `setInterval` returns an **ID**; store it in a variable so `clearInterval` can stop it. - ✅ `Math.floor` is the correct rounding for time/random indices — never use `Math.round` (causes bias).
